---
title: "graphviz 2.8"
date: 2006-03-20
forum: Ubuntu Backports
---

### Post by 18nikko on 2006-03-20
Hi,
I don't know if heare or the dapper development thread is the right place. 
Graphviz in debian is still at version 2.2 (several years old). The latest stable version of graphviz is 2.8. There are many desirable new features in the new version such as the cairo interface. I would be willing (with some coaching) to try and build the package. 

Nicholas

---

### Post by cilynx on 2006-03-20
```

sudo apt-get install devscripts

```

From there, read the doco.  I'm not a package developer, so that's about the best I can give you...

---

### Post by etitor on 2006-07-02
[QUOTE=18nikko]Hi,
I don't know if heare or the dapper development thread is the right place. 
Graphviz in debian is still at version 2.2 (several years old). The latest stable version of graphviz is 2.8. There are many desirable new features in the new version such as the cairo interface. I would be willing (with some coaching) to try and build the package. 

Nicholas[/QUOTE]
Hi Nicholas,

Under 5.10 I had managed somehow to have graphviz 2.8 running. I'm now on 6.06 and would like to give the update you mention to the Ubuntu community. Count me in if you find what should be done and how to do it.

For a starter, have a look at [http://packages.debian.org/unstable/graphics/graphviz.html](http://packages.debian.org/unstable/graphics/graphviz.html).

---

### Post by mlind on 2006-07-02
This was built directly from Debian upstream
[http://www.freefilehoster.com/uploads/1151864081graphviz-2.8.tar.gz](http://www.freefilehoster.com/uploads/1151864081graphviz-2.8.tar.gz)

---

### Post by etitor on 2006-07-03
[QUOTE=mlind]This was built directly from Debian upstream
[http://www.freefilehoster.com/uploads/1151864081graphviz-2.8.tar.gz](http://www.freefilehoster.com/uploads/1151864081graphviz-2.8.tar.gz)[/QUOTE]
Thanks mlind. It works!

### Post by Ziggurat on 2006-08-24
Hi, that package works fine but does not have cairo enabled, or at least i can find it.
Was anybody able to use graphviz-cairo on dapper?

Thanks.
